rationale: Weakly-informative. The channel-level prior on the true log10 IC50 is centered at 1 uM (m_0 = 3.0 log10 nM) with s_0 = 2.0 log10 nM, spanning roughly 10 pM to 100 uM, the full nM-uM range cardiac blockers occupy, so the prior is dominated by the data wherever the data identify the IC50. The between-lab spread hyperprior is HalfNormal with scale 0.5 log10 (~3.2-fold), the documented inter-laboratory / inter-platform IC50 spread (Kramer 2013; Elkins 2013; Crumb 2016); single-source channels borrow this learned spread instead of a hard-coded constant. The Hill prior is LogNormal(0, 0.3) on the dimensionless coefficient, weakly centered at 1 (mass on [0.55, 1.8]). The censored-block observation noise (block_sigma = 0.05 fractional block) sets how sharply a sub-saturating max-block measurement bounds the IC50 from below. No element of this prior encodes a risk conclusion; prior_sensitivity is reported per channel so any prior-dominated posterior is flagged (spec v0.2 sec 6, 7).
